# Generated by ollama (qwen3-coder_30b)
# Prompt: Write a Deno Deploy application that dynamically loads modules based on user configuration. Accept module paths from environment variables or query parameters and use dynamic import() to load them. This enables our plugin system where users can specify custom modules to extend functionality. The app runs on Deno Deploy edge runtime.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { serve } from "https://deno.land/std@0.180.0/http/server.ts";

interface Config {
  modulePath: string;
  moduleName: string;
}

const MODULE_PATH_ENV = "MODULE_PATH";
const MODULE_NAME_ENV = "MODULE_NAME";

function getConfigFromEnv(): Config {
  const modulePath = Deno.env.get(MODULE_PATH_ENV);
  const moduleName = Deno.env.get(MODULE_NAME_ENV);
  
  if (!modulePath) {
    throw new Error(`Environment variable ${MODULE_PATH_ENV} is required`);
  }
  
  return {
    modulePath,
    moduleName: moduleName || "default"
  };
}

function getConfigFromQuery(url: string): Config {
  const params = new URLSearchParams(new URL(url).search);
  const modulePath = params.get("modulePath");
  const moduleName = params.get("moduleName");
  
  if (!modulePath) {
    throw new Error("Query parameter modulePath is required");
  }
  
  return {
    modulePath,
    moduleName: moduleName || "default"
  };
}

async function loadModule(config: Config) {
  try {
    const module = await import(config.modulePath);
    return module;
  } catch (error) {
    throw new Error(`Failed to load module from ${config.modulePath}: ${error}`);
  }
}

async function handleRequest(request: Request): Promise<Response> {
  try {
    const url = new URL(request.url);
    let config: Config;
    
    // Prefer query parameters over environment variables
    if (url.searchParams.has("modulePath")) {
      config = getConfigFromQuery(request.url);
    } else {
      config = getConfigFromEnv();
    }
    
    const module = await loadModule(config);
    
    // Assume module exports a function called "execute"
    if (typeof module.execute === "function") {
      const result = await module.execute();
      return new Response(JSON.stringify(result), {
        headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" }
      });
    } else {
      return new Response(JSON.stringify({ message: "Module loaded successfully", data: module }), {
        headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" }
      });
    }
  } catch (error) {
    return new Response(JSON.stringify({ error: error.message }), {
      status: 500,
      headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" }
    });
  }
}

serve(handleRequest);
